comment_text.sn21.10:1.2: The suffix -nāmaka is a slight; see SN 10.2:1.2.
comment_text.sn21.10:2.2: The Buddha avoids -nāmaka and politely calls him “Senior”.
comment_text.sn21.10:3.6: Desire for “present incarnations” is in plural: desire for one’s own body and the bodies of others.

root_text.sn21.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn21.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu theranāmako ekavihārī ceva hoti ekavihārassa ca vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.sn21.10:1.3: So eko gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisati eko paṭikkamati eko raho nisīdati eko caṅkamaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.sn21.10:1.4: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn21.10:1.5: “idha, bhante, aññataro bhikkhu theranāmako ekavihārī ekavihārassa ca vaṇṇavādī”ti.
root_text.sn21.10:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn21.10:2.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena theraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantehi:
root_text.sn21.10:2.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yenāyasmā thero tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ theraṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.10:2.6: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā thero tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ theraṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.10:2.7: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, thera, ekavihārī ekavihārassa ca vaṇṇavādī”ti?
root_text.sn21.10:2.9: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, thera, ekavihārī ekavihārassa ca vaṇṇavādī”ti?
root_text.sn21.10:2.10: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, eko gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisāmi eko paṭikkamāmi eko raho nisīdāmi eko caṅkamaṁ adhiṭṭhāmi.
root_text.sn21.10:2.11: Evaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, ekavihārī ekavihārassa ca vaṇṇavādī”ti.
root_text.sn21.10:3.2: Api ca, thera, yathā ekavihāro vitthārena paripuṇṇo hoti
root_text.sn21.10:3.3: taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn21.10:3.6: Idha, thera, yaṁ atītaṁ taṁ pahīnaṁ, yaṁ anāgataṁ taṁ paṭinissaṭṭhaṁ, paccuppannesu ca attabhāvapaṭilābhesu chandarāgo suppaṭivinīto.
root_text.sn21.10:3.7: Evaṁ kho, thera, ekavihāro vitthārena paripuṇṇo hotī”ti.
root_text.sn21.10:4.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn21.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn21.10:1.2: Now at that time there was a certain mendicant who was Senior in name only. He lived alone and praised living alone.
translation_text.sn21.10:1.3: He entered the village for alms alone, returned alone, sat in private alone, and focussed on walking mindfully alone.
translation_text.sn21.10:1.4: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn21.10:1.5: “Sir, there’s a certain mendicant who is Senior in name only. He lives alone and praises living alone.”
translation_text.sn21.10:2.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.sn21.10:2.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell the mendicant Senior that
translation_text.sn21.10:2.4: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to Venerable Senior and said to him,
translation_text.sn21.10:2.6: “Yes, reverend,” that monk replied. He went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn21.10:2.7: “Is it really true, Senior, that you live alone and praise living alone?”
translation_text.sn21.10:2.9: “But in what way do you live alone and praise living alone?”
translation_text.sn21.10:2.10: “Well, sir, I enter the village for alms alone, return alone, sit in private alone, and focus on walking mindfully alone.
translation_text.sn21.10:2.11: That’s how I live alone and praise living alone.”
translation_text.sn21.10:3.1: “That is a kind of living alone, I don’t deny it.
translation_text.sn21.10:3.2: But as to how living alone is fulfilled in detail,
translation_text.sn21.10:3.3: listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn21.10:3.5: “And how, Senior, is living alone fulfilled in detail?
translation_text.sn21.10:3.6: It’s when what’s in the past is given up, what’s in the future is relinquished, and desire and greed for present incarnations is eliminated.
translation_text.sn21.10:3.7: That’s how living alone is fulfilled in detail.”
translation_text.sn21.10:4.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn21.10:4.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn21.10:5.2: is unsullied in the midst of all things.
translation_text.sn21.10:5.3: He’s given up all, freed in the ending of craving:
translation_text.sn21.10:5.4: I declare that man to be one who lives alone.”
